values.value:
== AUTH PROVIDER XML FORMAT == File path: authproviders/DeveloperName.authprovider metadataType: AuthProvider memberName: DeveloperName (no spaces, no __c — e.g. My_Google_Provider)
CRITICAL — ELEMENT ORDER: elements MUST appear in STRICT ALPHABETICAL ORDER. Salesforce rejects AuthProvider XML with wrong element order. Do NOT include <fullName> inside the XML body — the file name is the identity.
ALL PROVIDERS share these optional trailing elements (include only if needed): <customErrorUrl> — Custom Error URL (optional) <customLogoutUrl> — Custom Logout URL (optional) <iconUrl> — Icon URL (optional) <registrationHandler>ApexClassName</registrationHandler> (optional — Apex class name only, no quotes) <requireMfa>false</requireMfa> — defaults to false; set true only when ticket requests Salesforce MFA
CONSUMER SECRET RULE: consumerSecret is NEVER returned by any Salesforce API (stored encrypted). For CREATE or UPDATE: consumerSecret MUST come from the ticket. If the ticket does not provide it → return error in summary, do NOT deploy.
-- PROVIDER TYPE: Salesforce -- Use when: ticket says "Salesforce OAuth", "Salesforce Auth Provider", "Salesforce SSO" Required from ticket: consumerKey, consumerSecret <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?> <AuthProvider xmlns="http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata"> <authorizeUrl>https://login.salesforce.com/services/oauth2/authorize</authorizeUrl> <consumerKey>FROM_TICKET</consumerKey> <consumerSecret>FROM_TICKET</consumerSecret> <defaultScopes>api refresh_token</defaultScopes> <friendlyName>FROM_TICKET</friendlyName> <includeOrgIdInIdentifier>false</includeOrgIdInIdentifier> <isPkceEnabled>true</isPkceEnabled> <providerType>Salesforce</providerType> <requireMfa>false</requireMfa> <sendAccessTokenInHeader>false</sendAccessTokenInHeader> <sendClientCredentialsInHeader>false</sendClientCredentialsInHeader> <sendSecretInApis>true</sendSecretInApis> <tokenUrl>https://login.salesforce.com/services/oauth2/token</tokenUrl> </AuthProvider>
-- PROVIDER TYPE: Google -- Use when: ticket says "Google OAuth", "Google Auth Provider", "Google SSO", "Google Workspace" Required from ticket: consumerKey (Client ID), consumerSecret (Client Secret) <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?> <AuthProvider xmlns="http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata"> <authorizeUrl>https://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/auth</authorizeUrl> <consumerKey>FROM_TICKET</consumerKey> <consumerSecret>FROM_TICKET</consumerSecret> <defaultScopes>openid email profile</defaultScopes> <friendlyName>FROM_TICKET</friendlyName> <isPkceEnabled>true</isPkceEnabled> <providerType>Google</providerType> <requireMfa>false</requireMfa> <sendSecretInApis>true</sendSecretInApis> <tokenUrl>https://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/token</tokenUrl> <userInfoUrl>https://www.googleapis.com/oauth2/v3/userinfo</userInfoUrl> </AuthProvider>
-- PROVIDER TYPE: Microsoft -- Use when: ticket says "Microsoft OAuth", "Azure AD", "Microsoft Entra", "Microsoft SSO", "Azure OAuth" Required from ticket: consumerKey (Application/Client ID), consumerSecret (Client Secret) <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?> <AuthProvider xmlns="http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata"> <authorizeUrl>https://login.microsoftonline.com/common/oauth2/v2.0/authorize</authorizeUrl> <consumerKey>FROM_TICKET</consumerKey> <consumerSecret>FROM_TICKET</consumerSecret> <defaultScopes>openid email profile</defaultScopes> <friendlyName>FROM_TICKET</friendlyName> <isPkceEnabled>true</isPkceEnabled> <providerType>Microsoft</providerType> <requireMfa>false</requireMfa> <sendSecretInApis>true</sendSecretInApis> <!-- Include tokenIssuer ONLY when ticket provides a tenant-specific issuer --> <!-- <tokenIssuer>https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenantId}/v2.0</tokenIssuer> --> <tokenUrl>https://login.microsoftonline.com/common/oauth2/v2.0/token</tokenUrl> <userInfoUrl>https://graph.microsoft.com/oidc/userinfo</userInfoUrl> </AuthProvider>
-- PROVIDER TYPE: OpenIdConnect -- Use when: ticket says "Open ID Connect", "OpenID Connect", "OIDC", "Custom OAuth 2.0" <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?> <AuthProvider xmlns="http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata"> <authorizeUrl>FROM_TICKET</authorizeUrl> <consumerKey>FROM_TICKET</consumerKey> <consumerSecret>FROM_TICKET</consumerSecret> <defaultScopes>openid email profile</defaultScopes> <friendlyName>FROM_TICKET</friendlyName> <isPkceEnabled>true</isPkceEnabled> <providerType>OpenIdConnect</providerType> <requireMfa>false</requireMfa> <sendAccessTokenInHeader>true</sendAccessTokenInHeader> <sendClientCredentialsInHeader>false</sendClientCredentialsInHeader> <sendSecretInApis>true</sendSecretInApis> <tokenUrl>FROM_TICKET</tokenUrl> </AuthProvider>
-- PROVIDER TYPE: Facebook -- <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?> <AuthProvider xmlns="http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata"> <authorizeUrl>https://www.facebook.com/dialog/oauth</authorizeUrl> <consumerKey>FROM_TICKET</consumerKey> <consumerSecret>FROM_TICKET</consumerSecret> <defaultScopes>email</defaultScopes> <friendlyName>FROM_TICKET</friendlyName> <isPkceEnabled>true</isPkceEnabled> <providerType>Facebook</providerType> <requireMfa>false</requireMfa> <sendSecretInApis>true</sendSecretInApis> <tokenUrl>https://graph.facebook.com/oauth/access_token</tokenUrl> <userInfoUrl>https://graph.facebook.com/me</userInfoUrl> </AuthProvider>
-- PROVIDER TYPE: GitHub -- <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?> <AuthProvider xmlns="http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata"> <authorizeUrl>https://github.com/login/oauth/authorize</authorizeUrl> <consumerKey>FROM_TICKET</consumerKey> <consumerSecret>FROM_TICKET</consumerSecret> <defaultScopes>user</defaultScopes> <friendlyName>FROM_TICKET</friendlyName> <providerType>GitHub</providerType> <requireMfa>false</requireMfa> <sendSecretInApis>true</sendSecretInApis> <tokenUrl>https://github.com/login/oauth/access_token</tokenUrl> </AuthProvider>
-- PROVIDER TYPE: Twitter -- Note: Twitter uses OAuth 1.0a — no authorizeUrl/tokenUrl needed in XML <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?> <AuthProvider xmlns="http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata"> <consumerKey>FROM_TICKET</consumerKey> <consumerSecret>FROM_TICKET</consumerSecret> <friendlyName>FROM_TICKET</friendlyName> <providerType>Twitter</providerType> <requireMfa>false</requireMfa> <sendSecretInApis>true</sendSecretInApis> </AuthProvider>
-- PROVIDER TYPE: Apple -- Required from ticket: teamId, servicesId, keyId Note: The private key file must be uploaded manually after deployment. <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?> <AuthProvider xmlns="http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata"> <friendlyName>FROM_TICKET</friendlyName> <keyId>FROM_TICKET</keyId> <providerType>Apple</providerType> <requireMfa>false</requireMfa> <servicesId>FROM_TICKET</servicesId> <teamId>FROM_TICKET</teamId> </AuthProvider> After deploying Apple AuthProvider XML, the summary MUST include: "Apple private key file must be manually uploaded via Setup > Auth Providers > [Name] > Edit > Key."
== AUTH PROVIDER RULES ==
-- URL SUFFIX = DEVELOPER NAME -- When the ticket provides a "URL Suffix" field: - Use the URL Suffix value as the DeveloperName → memberName AND file name - Use the "Name" field value as <friendlyName> inside the XML - NEVER use the Name/friendlyName value as the memberName
-- ELEMENT ORDER -- Elements MUST be in STRICT ALPHABETICAL ORDER. Salesforce: authorizeUrl, consumerKey, consumerSecret, defaultScopes, friendlyName, includeOrgIdInIdentifier, isPkceEnabled, providerType, requireMfa, sendAccessTokenInHeader, sendClientCredentialsInHeader, sendSecretInApis, tokenUrl Microsoft/OpenIdConnect: same as Google but may include tokenIssuer (between sendSecretInApis and tokenUrl)
-- CREATE -- 1. Identify the correct providerType from the ticket 2. Use the matching template above with pre-filled URLs 3. Replace all FROM_TICKET placeholders with values from the ticket 4. consumerSecret MUST come from the ticket — if missing, return error in summary
-- UPDATE -- 1. Find the AP in existingAuthProviders in orgState by developerName 2. If NOT found in orgState → return error in summary, do NOT generate files 3. Regenerate COMPLETE XML using ALL existing values from orgState 4. Only change the specific field(s) the ticket requests 5. consumerSecret is NEVER in orgState. If ticket provides new one → include it. If not → OMIT entirely. Priority rule: TICKET VALUE always wins over orgState. orgState only used when ticket does NOT mention that field.
-- DELETE -- 1. Confirm the developerName exists in existingAuthProviders in orgState 2. If NOT found → return error in summary, do NOT generate destructiveFiles 3. Use the shorthand destructiveFiles entry: { "metadataType": "AuthProvider", "member": "DeveloperName" } 4. Do NOT generate any files[] entry for delete
-- NEVER -- - Never include <fullName> or <developerName> inside AuthProvider XML body - Never fabricate consumerKey, consumerSecret, teamId, servicesId, keyId - Never change providerType during an update (providerType is immutable after creation) - Never use <tokenEndpointUrl> — the correct element is <tokenUrl> - Never use <authorizeEndpointUrl> — the correct element is <authorizeUrl> - Never use <scope> — the correct element is <defaultScopes> - Never use <name> — the correct element for the display name is <friendlyName> - Never use the "Name" field value as the memberName — URL Suffix IS the DeveloperName - Never include <urlSuffix> — this element does not exist in AuthProvider metadata
Auth Provider XML for Salesforce, Google, Microsoft, Facebook, GitHub, Twitter, Apple etc.
